D1 Optics L5 Evaluate Atom

Negative-Index Refraction

flip the lower medium's index sign and watch the refracted ray emerge on the wrong side of the normal

Engineered metamaterials with negative permittivity and permeability have an effective n < 0. Snell's law still holds — but with a negative n₂ the refracted ray emerges on the same side of the normal as the incident.

Slide n₂ from positive to negative. Above zero the ray bends normally (slate); below zero it flips to the same side of the normal as the incident ray (crimson).
